seoTitle: Jones Island vs Jordan Springs — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Jones Island and Jordan Springs. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Jones Island vs Jordan Springs: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Jones Island and Jordan Springs.
heading: Jones Island vs Jordan Springs — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Jones Island, New South Wales and Jordan Springs, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Jordan Springs has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Jones Island (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Jones Island or Jordan Springs safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Jones Island has a flood risk score of 34/100, while Jordan Springs scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Jones Island scores 33/100 for bushfire risk. Jordan Springs scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Jones Island or Jordan Springs?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Jordan Springs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.52% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
